traces.content: The operating boundary stays quiet until accumulated pressure crosses a threshold and triggers a state switch.
A feedback loop returns the system response into the next input and reinforces the overload cycle.
Reserve capacity drains under repeated demand until resource depletion removes the remaining operating margin.
A buffer absorbs demand spikes and delays visible failure until hidden pressure escapes the buffer.
After the local pathway saturates, failure pressure spreads across adjacent channels and dilutes local control.
